zotero.abstractNote: Probabilistic numerical methods for ordinary differential equations (ODEs) quantify the uncertainty arising from the discretization of the continuous-time problem. However, existing methods are computationally expensive, typically scaling cubically with the dimension of the ODE. This limits their application to small-scale problems. We propose a new class of probabilistic ODE solvers that scale linearly with the dimension of the ODE. We achieve this by using a Kronecker-factored structure for the covariance of the Gaussian process prior. We demonstrate that our method is competitive with state-of-the-art deterministic solvers in terms of accuracy and computational cost, while providing well-calibrated uncertainty estimates in millions of dimensions.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 39th International Conference on Machine Learning
